page: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-48GB is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 32 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with a single thread per core on a shared allocation model. It is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.5 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a bundled 200 GB local SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is positioned in the bottom 10%, reflecting the moderate 2.0 GHz clock speed. However, multi-core execution scales effectively, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores landing in the middle 50%. Notably, the server demonstrates strong performance in the top 25% for multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, PassMark CPU extended instructions, and PassMark CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain within average parameters.

The primary tradeoff of this configuration is the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.5 GB per core, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory processing. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelizable, compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive RAM footprints. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded compilation, cryptography, parallel data decompression, web serving, and moderately sized batch processing tasks.
description: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-48GB is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 32 vCPUs and 48.0 GB of RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7542 processor with x86_64 architecture. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance allocates 32 physical cores with one thread per core on a shared basis. It includes a 200 GB local SSD and one public IPv4 address, with no GPU acceleration. While single-core benchmarks are relatively weak, multi-core performance is solid, showing strong results in multi-threaded decompression, cryptography, and extended CPU instructions. The low memory-to-CPU ratio of 1.5 GB per core makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for highly parallelized, compute-bound workloads rather than memory-heavy applications. It is well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, and cryptographic processing.
og_description: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-48GB is a compute-optimized KVM virtual server featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a 200 GB local SSD, designed for parallelized workloads and cryptographic tasks.
meta_description: UpCloud HICPU-32xCPU-48GB is a KVM virtual server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 48 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
tagline: A high-core-count virtual server with bundled SSD storage, optimized for parallelized multi-threaded processing and cryptographic workloads.
bullet_points: 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs with 48 GB of system memory
Strong multi-threaded decompression and cryptographic performance
KVM hypervisor architecture with shared CPU allocation
Best suited for parallel compute tasks and web serving
